DATuM
  • beverli
  • View page source

beverli

This module defines the datum.beverli.Beverli class, which provides essential methods for querying the BeVERLI Hill geometry.

class datum.beverli.Beverli

Instances of the datum.beverli.Beverli class encapsulate the complete as-designed BeVERLI Hill geometry.

Each instance of this class is endowed with probing and visualization capabilities.

Variables:

  • cyl_section_width_m (float) – Width of the hill’s cylindrical section in units of meters.

  • height_m (float) – Height of the hill in units of meters.

  • geometry (Union[trimesh.base.Trimesh, Dict[numpy.ndarray]]) – The hill geometry as Trimesh object or dictionary of 2-D NumPy ndarrays of shape (n, m).

  • polynomial_coefficients (numpy.ndarray) – 1-D NumPy ndarray of shape (6,) containing the hill’s fifth-degree polynomial coefficients.

  • width_m (float) – Hill width in units of meters.

  • input_data (datum.my_types.InputData) – Nested dictionary containing the user input data.

compute_perimeter(hill_angle_deg)

Compute the hill’s perimeter.

Parameters:

hill_angle_deg (Real) – Yaw angle orientation of the hill, measured in degrees.

Return type:

Tuple[ndarray, ndarray]

Returns:

A tuple containing two 1-D NumPy ndarrays with shape (n,) representing the x 1 and x 3 coordinates of the hill, measured in meters, where n denotes the number of discrete perimeter points.

compute_x1_x2_profile(x_3_m)

Compute a cross-sectional x 1-x 2 profile of the hill at a specific x 3 location.

Parameters:

x_3_m (Real) – Location along the x :sub:3-direction of the cross-sectional profile, measured in meters.

Return type:

Tuple[ndarray, ndarray]

Returns:

A tuple containing two 1-D NumPy ndarrays with shape (n,) representing the x 1 and x 2 coordinates of the cross-sectional profile, measured in meters, where n denotes the number of discrete profile points.

probe_hill(x_1_m, x_3_m)

Probe the hill height at a specific (x 1, x 3) point.

Parameters:

  • x_1_m (Real) – The probe’s x 1 coordinate measured in meters.

  • x_3_m (Real) – The probe’s x 3 coordinate measured in meters.

Return type:

Real

Returns:

Height of the hill at the specified probe’s location.

_load_hill_geometry()

Load the hill geometry from the CAD or analytical model.

Return type:

Union[Trimesh, Dict[str, ndarray]]

Returns:

The hill geometry as a Trimesh object or a dictionary of 1-D NumPy ndarrays of shape (n,) representing the geometry’s Cartesian coordinates, where n denotes the number of available discrete points of the corresponding 3-D geometry point cloud.

_load_cad_hill()

Load the hill geometry from the CAD model.

Return type:

Trimesh

Returns:

The hill geometry as a Trimesh object.

_load_analytic_hill()

Load the hill geometry from the analytic model.

Return type:

Dict[str, ndarray]

Returns:

The hill geometry as a dictionary of 1-D NumPy ndarrays of shape (n,) representing the geometry’s Cartesian coordinates, where n denotes the number of available discrete points of the corresponding 3-D geometry point cloud.

_rotate_cad_hill(hill_geometry)

Rotate the CAD hill geometry about its x 2 axis.

Parameters:

hill_geometry (Trimesh) –

The hill geometry as a Trimesh object.

Return type:

Trimesh

Returns:

The rotated hill geometry as a Trimesh object.

_rotate_analytic_hill(hill_geometry)

Rotate the analytic hill geometry about its x 2 axis.

Parameters:

hill_geometry (Dict[str, ndarray]) – The hill geometry as a dictionary of 1-D NumPy ndarrays of shape (n,) representing the geometry’s Cartesian coordinates, where n denotes the number of available discrete points of the corresponding 3-D geometry point cloud.

Return type:

Dict[str, ndarray]

Returns:

The rotated hill geometry as a dictionary of 1-D NumPy ndarrays of shape (n,) representing the geometry’s Cartesian coordinates, where n denotes the number of available discrete points of the corresponding 3-D geometry point cloud.

_compute_polynomial_coefficients()

Compute the hill’s fifth-degree polynomial coefficients.

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

A 1-D NumPy ndarray of shape (6,) containing the polynomial coefficients.

_compute_fifth_degree_polynomial(x_1)

Probe the fifth-degree polynomial profile of the hill.

Parameters:

x_1 (Union[Real, ndarray]) – Cartesian coordinates of the probes in the x 1-direction as a single real number or a 1-D NumPy ndarray of shape (n,), where n denotes the number of probe points.

Return type:

Union[Real, ndarray]

Returns:

Cartesian coordinates of the profile in the x 2-direction at the specified probe points.

_compute_corner(u_p, v_p, sign_x_1, sign_x_3)

Construct one three-dimensional hill corner using the analytic parametrization (now obsolete) in a specified quadrant.

Parameters:

  • u_p (Union[Real, ndarray]) – First parameter of the parametric corner function as real number or a 1-D NumPy ndarray of shape (n,), where n denotes the number of discrete probing points.

  • v_p (Union[Real, ndarray]) – Second parameter of the parametric corner function as real number or a 1-D NumPy ndarray of shape (n,), where n denotes the number of discrete probing points.

  • sign_x_1 (Literal[-1, 1]) – Identifier for the quadrant of the hill corner in the x 1-direction, specified using -1 or 1.

  • sign_x_3 (Literal[-1, 1]) – Identifier for the quadrant of the hill corner in the x 3-direction, specified using -1 or 1.

Return type:

Tuple[Union[Real, ndarray], Union[Real, ndarray], Union[Real, ndarray]]

Returns:

A tuple of three real numbers or NumPy 1-D ndarrays or shape (n,), where n denotes the number of discrete probing points, representing the Cartesian coordinates of the hill corner in the specified quadrant.

_compute_flat_top(u_p, v_p)

Construct the flat top of the hill using the analytic parametrization.

Parameters:

  • u_p (Union[Real, ndarray]) – First parameter of the parametric function as a real number or a 1-D NumPy ndarray of shape (n,), where n denotes the number of discrete probing points.

  • v_p (Union[Real, ndarray]) – First parameter of the parametric function as a real number or a 1-D NumPy ndarray of shape (n,), where n denotes the number of discrete probing points.

Return type:

Tuple[Union[Real, ndarray], Union[Real, ndarray], Union[Real, ndarray]]

Returns:

A tuple of three real numbers or NumPy 1-D ndarrays or shape (n,), where n denotes the number of discrete probing points, representing the Cartesian coordinates of the hill top.

_compute_cyl_surface(u_p, v_p, quadrant)

Construct the cylindrical section of the hill using the analytic parametrization in a specified quadrant.

Parameters:

  • u_p (Union[Real, ndarray]) – First parameter of the parametric function as a real number or a 1-D NumPy ndarray of shape (n,), where n denotes the number of discrete probing points.

  • v_p (Union[Real, ndarray]) – Second parameter of the parametric function as a real number or a 1-D NumPy ndarray of shape (n,), where n denotes the number of discrete probing points.

  • quadrant (Literal[1, 2, 3, 4]) – The desired quadrant, specified using 1, 2, 3, or 4.

Return type:

Tuple[Union[Real, ndarray], Union[Real, ndarray], Union[Real, ndarray]]

Returns:

A tuple of three real numbers or NumPy 1-D ndarrays or shape (n,), where n denotes the number of discrete probing points, representing the Cartesian coordinates of the cylindrical section in the specified quadrant.

_probe_analytic_hill(x_1_probe_m, x_3_probe_m, hill_angle_degrees)

Probe the analytic (now obsolete) hill geometry using a single probe.

Parameters:

  • x_1_probe_m (Real) – The probe’s x 1 coordinate, measured in meters.

  • x_3_probe_m (Real) – The probe’s x 3 coordinate, measured in meters.

  • hill_angle_degrees (Real) – The yaw angle orientation of the hill, measured in degrees.

Return type:

Real

Returns:

The height of the Hill at the probe’s location, measured in meters.

_probe_cad_hill(x_1_m, x_3_m)

Probe the BeVERLI Hill CAD geometry at a single location using ray tracing.

Parameters:

  • x_1_m (Real) – The probe’s x 1 coordinate, measured in meters.

  • x_3_m (Real) – The probe’s x 3 coordinate, measured in meters.

Return type:

Real

Returns:

The height of the Hill at the probe’s location, measured in meters.

_compute_corners_perimeter(num_of_pts)

Compute the superelliptic corners of the hill perimeter.

Parameters:

num_of_pts (int) – An integer representing the number of discrete points used to discretize each corner.

Return type:

List[Tuple[ndarray, ndarray, ndarray]]

Returns:

A list of tuples of three 1-D NumPy ndarrays of shape (n,), where n denotes the number of discrete points, each array representing a Cartesian coordinate of a specific elliptic corner.

_calculate_edges_perimeter(num_of_pts)

Compute the straight edges of the hill perimeter.

Parameters:

num_of_pts (int) – Number of points to discretize each straight edge of the hill perimeter.

Return type:

List[Tuple[ndarray, int, ndarray]]

Returns:

A list of tuples of three 1-D NumPy ndarrays of shape (n,), where n denotes the number of discrete points, each array representing a Cartesian coordinate of a specific edge.

static _find_perimeter_intersection_points(x_1, x_3, x_3_intersect)

Find intersection points of hill’s perimeter with the x 1-x 2-plane at a desired probe point.

Parameters:

  • x_1 (ndarray) – A 1-D NumPy ndarray of shape (n,), where n denotes the number of discretization points, representing the hill perimeter’s x 1 coordinate.

  • x_3 (ndarray) – A 1-D NumPy ndarray of shape (n,), where n denotes the number of discretization points, representing the hill perimeter’s x 3 coordinate.

  • x_3_intersect (Real) – Desired x 3 location of the probing plane.

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

A 1-D NumPy ndarray of shape (2,) containing the two intersection points of the probing plane with the hill perimeter.
